q: A bag contains 5 red marbles, 3 green marbles, and 4 yellow marbles. If one marble is drawn at random, what is the probability that it is green?
A fair spinner is divided into 8 equal sectors numbered 1 through 8. What is the probability that the spinner lands on a multiple of 3?
explain: Multiples of 3 from 1 to 8 are {3, 6}, so there are 2 favorable outcomes out of 8. P = 2/8 = 1/4.

q: In how many different orders can 6 distinct trophies be arranged in a row on a shelf?
A box holds 10 light bulbs, of which 2 are defective. If one bulb is chosen at random, what is the probability that it is NOT defective?
Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers shown equals 5?
explain: There are 6 × 6 = 36 equally likely outcomes. Sums of 5 come from (1,4), (2,3), (3,2), (4,1) = 4 outcomes. P = 4/36 = 1/9.

q: A teacher must choose a committee of 3 students from a class of 9 students. In how many different ways can the committee be chosen?
explain: Order does not matter, so use combinations: 9C3 = (9 × 8 × 7)/(3 × 2 × 1) = 504/6 = 84.

q: A drawer contains 8 pens, of which 2 are out of ink. If 3 pens are drawn at random without replacement, what is the probability that at least one of them is out of ink?
explain: Use P(at least one) = 1 - P(none). P(none out of ink) = (6/8)(5/7)(4/6) = 120/336 = 5/14. So P(at least one) = 1 - 5/14 = 9/14.

q: In a group of 40 students, 22 take Chemistry, 18 take Biology, and 8 take both. If one student is selected at random, what is the probability that the student takes neither Chemistry nor Biology?
explain: Students taking at least one = 22 + 18 - 8 = 32. Students taking neither = 40 - 32 = 8. P(neither) = 8/40 = 1/5.

qa: The probability of getting at least one head
explain: P(at least one head) = 1 - P(no heads) = 1 - (1/2)3 = 1 - 1/8 = 7/8 = 0.875. Since 7/8 > 3/4, Quantity A is greater.

q: A box contains 4 white chips and 6 black chips. Three chips are drawn at random without replacement. What is the probability that all three chips are black? Give your answer as a decimal rounded to the nearest hundredth.
In a survey of 50 people, 30 like coffee, 25 like tea, and 12 like both. Select all statements that are true.
choices: 18 people like coffee but not tea
43 people like at least one of the two beverages
13 people like tea but not coffee
15 people like tea but not coffee
explain: Only coffee = 30 - 12 = 18 (true). Only tea = 25 - 12 = 13 (true, so the '15' option is false). At least one = 30 + 25 - 12 = 43 (true). Neither = 50 - 43 = 7 (true, so the '5' option is false). Correct: statements 1, 2, 3, and 4.
